failure_signature.details: This model's maximum context length is 128000 tokens. However, your messages resulted in 134522 tokens. Please reduce the length of the messages.

problem.goal_state: Run long-running LangChain agent with extensive conversation history

solutions.solution_summary: Implement conversation buffer window memory to limit context size
solution_steps: Replace ConversationBufferMemory with ConversationBufferWindowMemory(k=20) to keep only the last 20 message exchanges
Add a ConversationSummaryMemory as a secondary store that summarizes older messages using a cheaper model like gpt-4o-mini
Implement a token counting middleware using tiktoken to monitor context size before each LLM call
Set a safety threshold at 80% of the context window (102400 tokens) and trigger summarization when exceeded
Use LangChain's trim_messages utility to automatically trim the oldest messages when the window is full

solutions.solution_summary: Migrate to a model with a larger context window
solution_steps: Switch to claude-3.5-sonnet which supports 200K tokens, or gemini-1.5-pro which supports 1M tokens
Update the LangChain ChatModel instantiation to use the new model provider
Note that larger context windows may increase latency and cost, so combine with summarization for optimal results

failure_signature.details: MemoryError: Unable to allocate 8.5 GiB for embedding matrix. System has 7.8 GiB available.

problem.goal_state: Query large vector store with 2M+ documents for RAG-based research

solutions.solution_summary: Switch to a disk-backed or client-server vector store to reduce memory usage
solution_steps: Migrate from in-memory ChromaDB to persistent mode: chroma_client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path='/data/chroma_db')
For larger datasets, switch to a dedicated vector database like Qdrant, Weaviate, or Pinecone that handles large collections without loading everything into memory
If staying with ChromaDB, use the client-server architecture: run ChromaDB as a separate service and connect via HTTP client
Implement pagination for vector search results: query with limit=100 instead of retrieving all matching documents
Use dimensionality reduction (PCA or random projection) to reduce embedding size from 1536 to 512 dimensions, cutting memory by 66%

failure_signature.details: Input was truncated to fit within the 200000 token context window. 35% of tool output was dropped. Agent may produce incomplete or inaccurate results.

problem.goal_state: AutoGen coding agent reviewing large codebase and generating test suite

solutions.solution_summary: Implement selective context loading to include only relevant code segments
solution_steps: Instead of loading entire files, use AST parsing to extract only the functions and classes relevant to the current task
Implement a two-pass approach: first pass identifies relevant files using grep or embeddings, second pass loads only those sections
Configure AutoGen's message transforms to compress code blocks by removing comments and blank lines before sending to the LLM
Use a retrieval-augmented approach: store code in a vector database and retrieve only the most relevant snippets per query
Set up a hierarchical summarization pipeline that creates file-level and module-level summaries for navigation

failure_signature.details: Agent consumed 450000 total tokens across 12 iterations, exceeding the configured budget of 400000 tokens. Agent loop terminated early.

problem.goal_state: Complete multi-step data pipeline with budget-constrained token usage

solutions.solution_summary: Optimize token usage with caching and progressive summarization
solution_steps: Enable prompt caching to avoid re-sending identical system prompts and tool definitions on each iteration
Implement progressive summarization: after every 3 iterations, summarize the conversation history into a compact state representation
Use a cheaper model (gpt-4o-mini) for intermediate reasoning steps and reserve gpt-4o for final synthesis
Set per-iteration token limits: max_tokens=2048 for reasoning steps, max_tokens=4096 for final output
Add a token usage callback that logs per-iteration consumption to identify which steps are most expensive
